auth.forbid: Defense in depth: 1. src/oauth/token.ts — OAuth gate, fails closed when key is set. 2. infra/cloudflare/src/worker.ts — sanitizeEnv() rejects this key before forwarding env into the Cloudflare Sandbox. 3. .github/workflows/cloudflare-preview.yml — CI never sets it. Mint OAuth via `claude setup-token`; rotate via scripts/mint-claude-oauth-secret.ts (leak-safe mode-0o600 mktemp + stdin pipe to `wrangler secret put` and `gh secret set`).
auth.managed-agents-api: We ingest the managed-agents documentation and reuse its rubric / outcome pattern via the Messages API over OAuth. We do NOT call the managed-agents endpoints and we do NOT build managed-agent products.

primitive.name: /loop
primitive.purpose: self-pacing wakeup; prevents container sleep mid-batch

primitive.name: /goal
primitive.purpose: iterate until a completion condition is met

primitive.name: claude agents
primitive.purpose: local dispatch panel for in-process subagents

primitive.name: remote-control
primitive.purpose: operator's mobile entry point into this terminal session

primitive.name: claude-code-on-the-web
primitive.purpose: parallel cloud-VM sessions launched from mobile per dispatched unit

primitive.name: typescript-sdk
primitive.purpose: programmatic query() + startup() for long-lived orchestrators

primitive.name: programmatic-subagents
primitive.purpose: agents: { name: AgentDefinition } parameter for in-session fan-out

founder-primitives.p: Build for the next model, not today's. The model can already do things no product has yet exposed.
Pick languages, frameworks, and infra surfaces heavily represented in pretraining data. Default: TypeScript, React, Node, Terraform.
Long-running cron/loop agents beat one-shot prompts. The agent schedules, iterates, and reports back. "Loops are the future."
As the model improves, the harness shrinks. Push logic into model behavior, tools, and prompts rather than scaffolding.
Use only the public SDK + Messages API. No private endpoints, no internal-only headers. If something only works internally, file an issue and stop.
MCP / CLI / HTTP / computer-use are all just tokens to the model. Default to MCP; fall through to CLI, then HTTP, then computer-use.
One generalist agent with broad tools beats specialist sub-agents unless the task crosses a real domain boundary.
Don't make multi-quarter plans. Plan one iteration ahead; let the next model release reshape the plan.
Model power doesn't substitute for "build something people love." Detail work on UX, error messages, and dev ergonomics is still ~50% of success.
Give Opus 4.7 a measurable target and tell it to iterate until done. Frame every task as target + evaluation criterion, not a procedure.
Switching cost and process power weaken under AI. Network effects, scale, and cornered resources still hold. Bet on the latter.

founder-directives.d: For every task, first restate the goal as a measurable target plus a success predicate. Refuse to begin coding until you have written the predicate as either a test, a probe, or a `terraform plan` diff.
Default stack: TypeScript strict, Node 22 LTS, Terraform 1.8+, the `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` package, the Messages API (`/v1/messages`). Warn when a non-default language is requested.

Long-running work goes into a managed-agent (sub-agent registered with the orchestrator) with its own thread, retry budget, and cron. Short questions stay in-thread. Main thread never blocks on a long task.
Orchestration modules stay ≤300 LOC. If a module is growing, move logic into a tool definition or a system-prompt fragment instead of into orchestration code.
Every internal automation uses the same SDK surface a customer would. No private endpoints, no internal-only headers, no hidden flags. If something only works internally, stop and file an issue.
Tool ordering for any external system: (1) MCP server if one exists, (2) typed CLI, (3) HTTP API, (4) computer-use as a last resort. Record which tier was used in each tool result.
Children get narrow tool allowlists + deadlines + token budgets ≤ 200k. Parent reduces with explicit code, never "summarize my children" via the model.
Roadmaps are 7 days. At each Monday checkpoint, re-derive the next week from the last week's results. Past 2026-06-15, re-derive the auth and billing assumptions before continuing.
Every PR includes a one-line user-visible change note. If you cannot write that note, the change is internal-only and the PR title must start with `chore:` or `refactor:`.
When proposing architecture, name which of Helmer's seven powers the design is betting on. If the answer is "switching costs" or "process power," reconsider.

Every Anthropic-doc claim ends with `[cite: :
] `. A MISS triggers a catalogue update, never a web-search fallback.

push-notifications.rule: long-task completion + needs-input both ping the operator's phone

discipline.odd: Every phase from Phase 0 onward ships rubrics/phase-N.md modeled on vendor/anthropics/platform.claude.com/docs/en/managed-agents/define-outcomes.md. Every commit ends with the outcome ID: `(O<N>)`. Enforced for commits after 2026-05-15T04:30Z by src/lib/conventions.test.ts.
discipline.citations: Every new test file ships with a /** @cite path/under/vendor|seeds|rubrics/... */ header. CI guard: scripts/lib/citation-guard.ts. In-repo cross-references (docs/, README.md) belong in prose, NOT in @cite lines.
discipline.seed-vs-spec: Operator prompts are seeds, not specs. When a directive in an operator prompt conflicts with a cited doc, the cited doc wins and the conflict is logged in the PR description. When two operator directives conflict, the more restrictive auth posture wins.
discipline.silent-failure-policy: No silent fallbacks. If a verify gate fails, stop and surface the error; do not paper over with a generic catch. pr-babysitter routine inspects every automerge-labeled PR's checks and classifies failures (flakey vs real); ci-healer routine reruns flakies and escalates real failures to the operator's mobile.

code-standards.rule: New behavior lands as `src/lib/<name>.ts` with a sibling test. Scripts under `scripts/` are thin entry points that import from `src/lib/` — they do not own logic that another script could reuse.
tsconfig.json runs `strict: true`. New exports declare explicit types. No `any` outside narrowly-scoped vendor-SDK shims.
infra/cloudflare/src/outbound-allowlist.ts is append-only. New vendor hosts join the list; existing entries are never removed without a separate PR + rubric.

Secret rotation NEVER prints the secret to stdout. Mint to mode-0o600 mktemp, pipe via stdin to `wrangler secret put` / `gh secret set`, shred the file. Canonical impl: scripts/mint-claude-oauth-secret.ts; mirrored in scripts/mint-neon-api-secret.ts.
Every test file ships an @cite header. Vendor client wrappers (src/lib/<vendor>-client.ts) include a top-of-file comment pointing at the vendor doc that drove the shape.

routine.name: pr-babysitter
routine.purpose: scan automerge-labeled PRs, classify CI failures, dispatch ci-fixer

routine.name: ci-healer
routine.purpose: distinguish flakey reruns from real failures; escalate

execution.long-arc: Workers-resident agents writing the codebase end-to-end (founder pattern pattern). scripts/orchestrator.ts is the first concrete step: a long-lived TypeScript orchestrator that fans out to 5 in-process subagents (vendor-bootstrapper, runbook-writer, ci-fixer, citation-validator, smoke-tester) and dispatches cloud-session paste-prompts for parallelizable vendor bootstraps.

doc.drives: mobile entry point into this session
